steps.title: Match the comparison
steps.content: The opening phrase compares something to "that of alumina glass." Here, that refers to alumina glass's atomic arrangement, so the noun after the comma must be silica glass's atomic arrangement, not silica glass itself or a disadvantage.

steps.title: Reject dangling subjects
steps.content: A and B put silica glass after the comparison, illogically comparing a substance to an atomic arrangement. C puts a significant disadvantage in that position, also creating an illogical comparison. Only the choice that begins with silica glass's atomic arrangement keeps the comparison parallel. The answer is D.
